purpose: Single source of truth linking OEM/diagnostic terminology to names used by locksmith tools.

naming_rules.official_name: Use only when supported by OEM or reliable diagnostic documentation.
naming_rules.locksmith_name: Common trade/tool name; may not be an OEM designation.
naming_rules.tool_menu_names: Exact menu wording must be captured from the named tool/version and supported by evidence.
naming_rules.aliases: Search terms only; aliases must never be treated as proof that two systems are identical.

vw_immo_1.architecture: Separate immobilizer control module or immobilizer integrated in instrument cluster, depending on vehicle.

vw_immo_2.architecture: Separate immobilizer control module or instrument-cluster integrated implementation, depending on vehicle.

vw_immo_3.architecture: Vehicle-specific implementation; do not infer from model year alone.

vw_immo_4a_adaptation.architecture: Generation 4 implementation identified in diagnostic documentation as using adaptation rather than a revolving challenge.

vw_immo_4_challenge.architecture: Generation 4 implementation that exposes a changing immobilizer challenge value; exact locksmith-tool route is tool and cluster dependent.

vw_immo_4d_passat.architecture: Immobilizer integrated in Comfort Control Module J393 on the documented Passat 3C implementation.

vw_kessy_early_non_encrypted.official_name: Access/Start Authorization - exact control-unit designation vehicle dependent
vw_kessy_early_non_encrypted.architecture: Practical locksmith split for KESSY modules commonly read through a discrete serial EEPROM. Vehicle, part-number and PCB identification are mandatory before inheritance.
research_notes: Field classification supplied by a working UK auto locksmith.
Do not classify by model year alone.

vw_kessy_late_encrypted.official_name: Access/Start Authorization - exact control-unit designation vehicle dependent
vw_kessy_late_encrypted.architecture: Practical locksmith split for encrypted KESSY modules requiring a dedicated solder-free adapter or verified wired bench connection. Exact hardware must be identified before procedure selection.
research_notes: Field classification supplied by a working UK auto locksmith.
Do not classify by model year alone.

vw_immo_45.official_name_status: No single OEM designation verified yet; retain as locksmith/tool classification until mapped to a documented implementation.
vw_immo_45.architecture: Trade classification requiring separation by cluster/module type before vehicle inheritance is allowed.

vw_immo_5.architecture: Vehicle-specific implementation; do not treat MQB, BCM2 or all keyless systems as automatically equivalent to Immo 5.

vw_mqb_48.official_name: MQB platform immobilizer - exact OEM generation name vehicle dependent
vw_mqb_48.architecture: Locksmith classification. Platform mapping must include cluster type, key type and procedure evidence.

vw_mqb_49_5c.official_name: MQB platform immobilizer - exact OEM generation name vehicle dependent
vw_mqb_49_5c.architecture: Locksmith/tool classification. MQB49 and 5C must remain separately searchable and must not be merged without vehicle/tool evidence.

vw_mlb_bcm2.architecture: On documented MLB applications, J393 BCM2 performs immobilizer/KESSY functions and uses diagnostic address 05 for access/start authorization.

vw_mlb_evo.official_name: MLB evo immobilizer system - exact control-unit designation vehicle dependent
vw_mlb_evo.architecture: Must remain distinct from BCM2 1.1/2.0 unless documentation proves compatibility.

vw_meb.official_name: MEB platform access/start authorization - exact control-unit designation vehicle dependent
vw_meb.architecture: Electric-vehicle platform classification; no programming route may be inherited until UK tool and OEM evidence is recorded.

vw_crafter_derived.architecture: Placeholder family boundary only. First- and second-generation Crafter systems must be researched separately.
